all.en: Every Friday night, the Chen family gathers around the dining table for a special meal.
all.zh: 每個星期五晚上，陳家人都會圍著餐桌享用一頓特別的晚餐。

all.en: Mom serves steaming bowls of beef noodle soup while Dad pours tea for everyone.
all.zh: 媽媽端上熱騰騰的牛肉麵湯，爸爸則為大家倒茶。

all.en: "So, who wants to share their week first?" Dad asks with a warm smile.
all.zh: 「那麼，誰想先分享這週的事？」爸爸帶著溫暖的微笑問道。

all.en: Emily, the eldest, announces that she finally got promoted to assistant manager at her office.
all.zh: 大女兒艾蜜莉宣布她終於在公司升為助理經理了。

all.en: Everyone cheers and her younger brother Kevin gives her a playful high-five.
all.zh: 大家歡呼起來，弟弟凱文還跟她俏皮地擊了個掌。

all.en: Kevin then complains about his math teacher, who assigned three chapters of homework over the weekend.
all.zh: 接著凱文抱怨他的數學老師，週末出了三章的作業。

all.en: Mom laughs and reminds him that complaining won't make the homework disappear.
all.zh: 媽媽笑著提醒他，抱怨並不會讓作業消失。

all.en: Grandma, sitting at the head of the table, shares that she joined a senior dance class on Wednesday.
all.zh: 坐在主位的奶奶分享說她週三加入了一個長青舞蹈班。

all.en: "At seventy-two, I can still move my hips," she says proudly, making everyone laugh.
all.zh: 「七十二歲了，我的腰還是能扭呢，」她得意地說，惹得大家哈哈大笑。

all.en: Dad mentions that his company is sending him to Tokyo for a conference next month.
all.zh: 爸爸提到下個月公司要派他去東京參加會議。

all.en: By the end of dinner, everyone feels closer, knowing what's happening in each other's lives.
all.zh: 晚餐結束時，大家都感到更親近了，因為了解了彼此的近況。

all.en: Emily volunteers to do the dishes, and Kevin actually offers to help dry them.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉自願洗碗，凱文居然也主動幫忙擦乾。

all.en: After the dishes were done, the family moved to the living room to watch a movie together.
all.zh: 碗洗好後，全家移到客廳一起看電影。

all.en: Kevin let Grandma choose this week, so they ended up with an old black-and-white classic.
all.zh: 凱文讓奶奶這週挑片，結果他們選了一部老的黑白經典電影。

all.en: Grandma explained the story to Kevin, who pretended to be bored but secretly enjoyed it.
all.zh: 奶奶向凱文解釋劇情，他假裝無聊，但其實偷偷看得很投入。

all.en: Emily curled up on the couch with a blanket and slowly fell asleep during the second hour.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉裹著毯子蜷在沙發上，看到第二個小時就慢慢睡著了。

all.en: Dad brought out a tray of sliced fruit and tea cups for everyone.
all.zh: 爸爸端出一盤切好的水果和茶杯給大家。

all.en: Mom whispered to Dad that she felt grateful these Friday nights still happened every week.
all.zh: 媽媽悄悄對爸爸說，她很感激這些週五夜晚每週都還能繼續。

all.en: Kevin asked Grandma to teach him a few dance steps from her senior class.
all.zh: 凱文央求奶奶教他幾個老人舞蹈班學的舞步。

all.en: Grandma laughed and pulled him up, demonstrating a slow waltz right in the living room.
all.zh: 奶奶笑著把他拉起來，當場在客廳示範了一段慢華爾滋。

all.en: Before going to bed, Emily reminded everyone that next Friday she would cook for the family.
all.zh: 睡前，艾蜜莉提醒大家下週五輪到她下廚。

all.en: Dad turned off the lights and smiled, thinking how lucky he was to have such a warm family.
all.zh: 爸爸關了燈，微笑著想著自己擁有這麼溫暖的家庭真是幸運。

all.en: The next Friday came quickly, and Emily started cooking right after she got home from work.
all.zh: 下個星期五很快就到了，艾蜜莉一下班回家就開始下廚。

all.en: She had chosen to make a Japanese-style curry with chicken, carrots, and soft boiled potatoes.
all.zh: 她決定煮日式咖哩，加了雞肉、紅蘿蔔和燉得鬆軟的馬鈴薯。

all.en: Kevin walked into the kitchen and offered to chop the onions, claiming his eyes never watered.
all.zh: 凱文走進廚房，自願切洋蔥，宣稱自己的眼睛從來不會流淚。

all.en: Within minutes, he was wiping tears with his sleeve, and Grandma laughed from the doorway.
all.zh: 沒過幾分鐘，他就用袖子擦眼淚了，奶奶在門口看了哈哈大笑。

all.en: Dad set the table carefully and lit a small candle in the center for a cozy feeling.
all.zh: 爸爸細心地擺好餐具，並在桌子中央點了一根小蠟燭，營造溫馨的氣氛。

all.en: Mom poured fruit juice for the children and a glass of red wine for the adults.
all.zh: 媽媽為孩子們倒了果汁，給大人倒了一杯紅酒。

all.en: Emily proudly served the curry and waited nervously for everyone's first reaction.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉得意地端上咖哩，緊張地等著大家第一口的反應。

all.en: Grandma took a careful bite and declared that the flavor reminded her of a trip to Osaka.
all.zh: 奶奶仔細嚐了一口，宣布這味道讓她想起一趟大阪之旅。

all.en: Everyone agreed that Emily had truly stepped up and earned the title of family chef this week.
all.zh: 大家一致同意艾蜜莉這週確實表現出色，當之無愧贏得家庭主廚的頭銜。

all.en: That night, Mom started a notebook to record every Friday meal so they would never forget any.
all.zh: 那晚，媽媽開始準備一本筆記本，記下每個週五的菜色，這樣就不會忘記任何一道。

all.en: By the following Friday, Mom had already filled three pages of her new family dinner notebook.
all.zh: 到了下個星期五，媽媽已經在新的家庭晚餐筆記本上寫了三頁。

all.en: Grandma offered to cook this week and announced she would prepare her famous braised pork rice.
all.zh: 奶奶這週主動表示要下廚，並宣布要準備她的招牌滷肉飯。

all.en: Kevin set the table while Emily helped Grandma chop ginger and green onions in the kitchen.
all.zh: 凱文擺餐具，艾蜜莉則在廚房幫奶奶切薑和蔥。

all.en: Dad opened a bottle of plum wine that he had been saving for a special weekend occasion.
all.zh: 爸爸開了一瓶他特地留著等週末特別場合喝的梅酒。

all.en: The aroma of slow-cooked pork filled every corner of the house and even drifted into the garden.
all.zh: 慢燉豬肉的香味飄滿了家裡每個角落，甚至飄進了花園。

all.en: Grandma told stories of cooking the same dish for Dad when he was just a little boy.
all.zh: 奶奶分享起她當年為還是小男孩的爸爸煮這道菜的故事。

all.en: Kevin asked if he could learn to make the dish himself, surprising the entire family at the table.
all.zh: 凱文問他能不能學著做這道菜，讓全家人都在餐桌前驚訝不已。

all.en: Emily proudly recorded the recipe in Mom's notebook with neat handwriting and a small drawing.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉得意地用工整的字跡和一張小插圖把食譜記在媽媽的筆記本裡。

all.en: After dinner, Dad shared photos from his recent business trip and described the streets of Tokyo.
all.zh: 晚餐後，爸爸分享他最近出差的照片，描述了東京的街道。

all.en: Before bed, Mom whispered that this Friday tradition was now her favorite part of every week.
all.zh: 睡前，媽媽悄悄說這個星期五的傳統如今是她每週最喜歡的時光。

all.en: Kevin spent the next Friday preparing simple grilled fish under Grandma's careful guidance.
all.zh: 凱文下個星期五在奶奶細心指導下，準備了簡單的烤魚。

all.en: Emily quietly recorded each cooking step in Mom's notebook so the family could remember it later.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉默默把每個烹飪步驟記在媽媽的筆記本裡，方便家人日後翻閱。

all.en: Dad opened a bottle of light white wine that paired perfectly with the gentle flavors of the dish.
all.zh: 爸爸開了一瓶清爽的白酒，搭配這道清淡口味的菜肴恰到好處。

all.en: Mom complimented Kevin so warmly that his cheeks turned bright pink at the dinner table.
all.zh: 媽媽溫暖地稱讚凱文，他坐在餐桌前臉頰都紅得發燙了。

all.en: Grandma added a small note in the notebook about her late husband's favorite seasoning trick.
all.zh: 奶奶在筆記本裡加了一段小註記，寫下她已故先生最愛用的調味小技巧。

all.en: After dinner, the family looked through old travel photos that Dad had brought back from Tokyo.
all.zh: 晚餐後，全家一起翻看爸爸從東京帶回來的舊旅遊照片。

all.en: Emily suggested they begin saving for a future family trip to Japan during the summer holiday.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉提議大家開始存錢，未來暑假可以全家一起去日本旅遊。

all.en: Kevin enthusiastically agreed and immediately offered to contribute part of his allowance every week.
all.zh: 凱文興奮地同意，當場主動表示要每週貢獻一部分零用錢。

all.en: Grandma promised to teach the family a few basic Japanese phrases before they ever boarded the plane.
all.zh: 奶奶答應在他們上飛機前，會教全家幾句基本的日語會話。

all.en: That night, Mom closed her notebook gently, knowing it now held the heart of their weekly tradition.
all.zh: 那晚，媽媽輕輕闔上筆記本，心知這本本子如今承載了他們每週傳統的核心。

all.en: The next Friday dinner, Grandma taught everyone how to say good evening and thank you in Japanese.
all.zh: 下個週五的晚餐，奶奶教大家用日文說晚安和謝謝。

all.en: Kevin practiced bowing politely and made Emily laugh until she nearly spilled her noodle soup.
all.zh: 凱文認真練習鞠躬，把艾蜜莉逗得差點把麵湯灑出來。

all.en: Mom started a small glass jar on the kitchen counter labeled simply Japan Trip Savings.
all.zh: 媽媽在廚房檯面上放了一個小玻璃罐，上面只寫著日本旅遊基金。

all.en: Every Friday, each family member dropped a few coins or a folded bill into the growing jar.
all.zh: 每個週五，全家人都會在罐子裡投入幾枚硬幣或一張對折的鈔票，存款慢慢累積。

all.en: Dad surprised everyone by booking a Japanese cooking class so they could practice meals before the trip.
all.zh: 爸爸給大家一個驚喜，預約了一堂日本料理課，讓全家在出發前先練習做菜。

all.en: Grandma proudly rolled her first sushi at the class and posed cheerfully for a family photo.
all.zh: 奶奶在課堂上得意地捲出第一條壽司，還開心地擺姿勢拍了全家福。

all.en: Emily started a small notebook listing every famous park and temple she wanted the family to visit.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉開了一本小筆記本，列出她希望全家造訪的每座著名公園與寺廟。

all.en: Kevin practiced reading hiragana every night and proudly showed Grandma his messy but improving handwriting.
all.zh: 凱文每晚練習讀平假名，得意地把字跡雖然凌亂卻越來越好的本子拿給奶奶看。

all.en: When summer arrived, the savings jar was finally heavy enough to cover four discounted plane tickets.
all.zh: 夏天到來時，存錢罐終於沉甸甸地足以支付四張優惠機票。

all.en: On the morning of the flight, Mom hugged her notebook tightly, knowing a new family chapter was beginning.
all.zh: 搭機當天的早晨，媽媽緊緊抱著她的筆記本，明白家裡新的篇章正要展開。

all.en: When the plane landed in Tokyo, Kevin practiced his bowing again and proudly greeted the airport staff in Japanese.
all.zh: 飛機降落東京時，凱文又開始練習鞠躬，得意地用日語向機場人員打招呼。

all.en: Grandma led the family to her favorite ramen shop, which she had remembered from her trip many years ago.
all.zh: 奶奶帶全家去她最愛的拉麵店，那是她多年前旅行時記下來的店。

all.en: Emily checked items off her park and temple notebook while Dad photographed each location for future memories.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉一一勾掉公園與寺廟筆記本上的景點，爸爸則為每個地方拍下照片留作回憶。

all.en: Kevin successfully ordered takoyaki entirely in Japanese, beaming as the vendor complimented his clear pronunciation.
all.zh: 凱文全程用日語成功點了章魚燒，攤販稱讚他發音清楚，他笑得合不攏嘴。

all.en: Mom carefully recorded every meal in her notebook, including drawings of unfamiliar ingredients she wanted to try at home.
all.zh: 媽媽細心地把每頓飯記進筆記本，還畫下了想回家試做的陌生食材。

all.en: On the fourth evening, the family attended a traditional tea ceremony arranged by their hotel concierge.
all.zh: 第四天傍晚，全家參加了由飯店禮賓安排的傳統茶道體驗。

all.en: Grandma whispered to Kevin that she had never imagined returning to Japan with her grandchildren by her side.
all.zh: 奶奶悄悄對凱文說，她從未想過能和孫子孫女一起重遊日本。

all.en: Back in Taipei the following Friday, Emily cooked miso soup using a recipe she had learned during the trip.
all.zh: 下個星期五回到台北後，艾蜜莉用旅行中學到的食譜煮了一鍋味噌湯。

all.en: Mom added a thick new section to the notebook titled Our Japan Memories, with photos taped between every page.
all.zh: 媽媽在筆記本裡新增了厚厚一章，標題寫著「我們的日本回憶」，每頁之間都貼上了照片。

all.en: That night, the family agreed to start saving again, this time for a future trip to Kyoto in the spring.
all.zh: 那晚，全家決定再次開始存錢，這次的目標是明年春天的京都之旅。

all.en: The very next Friday, Mom placed a fresh glass jar beside the old one and labeled it Kyoto.
all.zh: 下個星期五，媽媽在舊罐子旁邊放了一個新的玻璃罐，並貼上「京都」的標籤。

all.en: Kevin emptied his piggy bank into the new jar to set a generous example for everyone else.
all.zh: 凱文把自己的存錢筒倒進新罐子裡，為其他人做了一個慷慨的榜樣。

all.en: Emily printed photos of famous Kyoto temples and taped them onto the side of the glass jar.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉印了幾張京都名寺的照片，貼在玻璃罐的側面。

all.en: Grandma began telling stories about her own grandmother, who had once lived briefly near Kyoto.
all.zh: 奶奶開始講起她祖母的故事，那位祖母曾經短暫地住在京都附近。

all.en: Dad surprised everyone by enrolling the family in a weekend Japanese conversation class together.
all.zh: 爸爸給大家一個驚喜，幫全家報名了一堂週末的日語會話課。

all.en: Kevin caught on quickly and soon started teaching Grandma the modern slang he had picked up online.
all.zh: 凱文學得很快，沒多久就開始教奶奶他在網路上學到的現代俚語。

all.en: Mom recorded each new phrase in her notebook so the family could review them during dinner conversations.
all.zh: 媽媽把每個新句子記進筆記本裡，方便全家在晚餐聊天時複習。

all.en: Emily volunteered to research traditional Kyoto restaurants that would welcome a multi-generational family of four.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉自願去查能歡迎四代同堂家庭用餐的傳統京都餐廳。

all.en: By the end of autumn, the Kyoto jar already held more coins than the Tokyo jar ever had.
all.zh: 到了秋末，京都罐裡的硬幣數量已經超過當初的東京罐了。

all.en: On the last Friday of the year, Dad raised his glass and toasted to many more family adventures ahead.
all.zh: 那一年的最後一個星期五，爸爸舉杯敬酒，祝全家未來有更多精彩的旅程。

all.en: On the first Friday of the new year, Kevin accidentally burned the rice and the smoke alarm screamed for almost a minute.
all.zh: 新年第一個星期五，凱文不小心把飯燒焦了，火災警報器響了快一分鐘。

all.en: Grandma laughed so hard she had to sit down, and Mom opened every window to chase out the smoky smell.
all.zh: 奶奶笑到必須坐下來，媽媽把每扇窗戶都打開，想趕走那股焦味。

all.en: They ended up ordering takeout dumplings, and Dad joked that even kitchen disasters made better stories than perfect dinners.
all.zh: 他們最後改點了外送水餃，爸爸開玩笑說連廚房災難都比完美的晚餐更值得拿來講。

all.en: Kevin promised to take a cooking class before his next turn, and Emily quietly added his name to a weekend course she had been eyeing.
all.zh: 凱文答應下次輪到他下廚前要先去上烹飪課，艾蜜莉默默把他的名字填進她早就看上的週末課程。

all.en: The Kyoto jar had grown so heavy that Mom had to move it from the kitchen counter to a sturdy shelf in the dining room.
all.zh: 京都罐已經重到媽媽必須把它從廚房檯面移到餐廳裡比較穩固的層架上。

all.en: Grandma started slipping in old gold coins she had saved from her own wedding gifts decades ago.
all.zh: 奶奶開始悄悄放入她幾十年前結婚時收到的舊金幣。

all.en: Emily admitted that she had memorized twenty-three Japanese verbs by writing them on Post-its stuck to her bathroom mirror.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉坦承她把日文動詞寫在便利貼上貼浴室鏡子，已經背了二十三個。

all.en: Kevin proposed a family bet over who could correctly use the most new Japanese phrases during one Friday dinner.
all.zh: 凱文提議全家打賭，看誰能在一個星期五的晚餐裡正確使用最多新的日文片語。

all.en: Dad lost by mixing up the words for hot and cold, and he had to do the dishes for two Fridays as the agreed penalty.
all.zh: 爸爸因為把「熱」和「冷」搞混而輸了，依照約定要連洗兩個星期五的碗盤。

all.en: On the night they finally booked the Kyoto flights, Mom hugged her notebook so tightly that a faint pineapple cake stain transferred onto the cover.
all.zh: 終於訂下京都機票那晚，媽媽把筆記本抱得太緊，一塊淡淡的鳳梨酥污漬印到了封面上。

all.en: The smell of garlic and ginger filled the whole kitchen.
all.zh: 蒜頭和薑的香味瀰漫了整個廚房。

all.en: My little brother always grabs the last dumpling before anyone notices.
all.zh: 我弟弟總是在大家發現前就搶走最後一顆水餃。

all.en: Dad told a funny story about his boss that made us all laugh.
all.zh: 爸爸講了一個關於他老闆的趣事，逗得我們全笑了。

all.en: We take turns washing the dishes after every Friday meal.
all.zh: 每次週五的晚餐後，我們會輪流洗碗。

all.en: Grandma insists that homemade soup tastes better than anything from a restaurant.
all.zh: 外婆堅持自己煮的湯比任何餐廳的都好喝。

all.en: Mom always sets an extra bowl in case a neighbor drops by.
all.zh: 媽媽總會多擺一個碗，以防鄰居來訪。

all.en: We turn off our phones during dinner so we can really talk.
all.zh: 吃晚餐時我們會關掉手機，這樣才能好好聊天。

all.en: My sister shared the good news that she finally got her dream job.
all.zh: 姐姐分享了好消息，她終於得到了夢寐以求的工作。

all.en: After dinner, we usually sit on the sofa and watch an old movie.
all.zh: 晚餐後，我們通常坐在沙發上看一部老電影。

all.en: These simple family dinners are the moments I treasure the most.
all.zh: 這些簡單的家庭聚餐，是我最珍惜的時刻。
